title: hieracium piloselloides subsp. floccosum
intro: Hieracium piloselloides subsp. floccosum, also known as woolly hawk's-beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Consuming Hieracium piloselloides subsp. floccosum has been associated with various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which may help in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
